rationale.R1.1: The delegation is 'Improve this draft,' which implies fixing the contribution claim — the artifact's core framing is internally contradictory (it claims multi-agent collaboration yet describes a single shared network with a summed gradient update), so the thesis and gap statement require reconstruction to be coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the draft contains a direct logical inconsistency — the architecture section describes one monolithic network θ updated by summed gradients, which flatly contradicts the 'specialized agents resolving competing objectives' framing. This cross-section incoherence must be resolved.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the empirical claims are heterogeneous and incomparable (L2 error on Burgers vs. BC-residual reduction on Allen–Cahn; no wall-clock, parameter count, or convergence-step baselines), and the ablation supports only one of three λ terms. Alignment between claims and evidence must be tightened.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim (multi-agent perspective on PINN objectives) needs sharpening against prior work on gradient-surgery, PCGrad, and loss-balancing in PINNs — the draft cites only Wang et al. (2021) in passing and does not differentiate the contribution clearly.
rationale.R1.6: General prose improvement is explicitly requested ('Improve this draft'); sentence-level clarity, hedging calibration, and paragraph cohesion all need attention across the full draft.
